2604.12306.title: GCA Framework: A Gulf-Grounded Dataset and Agentic Pipeline for Climate Decision Support
2604.12306.paper_summary: Climate decision-making in the Gulf increasingly demands systems that can translate heterogeneous scientific and policy evidence into actionable guidance, yet general-purpose large language models (LLMs) remain weak both in region-specific climate knowledge and grounded interaction with geospatial and forecasting tools. We present the GCA framework, which unifies (i) GCA-DS, a curated Gulf-focused multimodal dataset, and (ii) Gulf Climate Agent (GCA), a tool-augmented agent for climate analysis. GCA-DS comprises ~200k question-answer pairs spanning governmental policies and adaptation plans, NGO and international frameworks, academic literature, and event-driven reporting on heatwaves, dust storms, and floods, complemented with remote-sensing inputs that couple imagery with textual evidence. Building on this foundation, the GCA agent orchestrates a modular tool pipeline grounded in real-time and historical signals and geospatial processing that produces derived indices and interpretable visualizations. Finally, we benchmark open and proprietary LLMs on Gulf climate tasks and show that domain fine-tuning and tool integration substantially improve reliability over general-purpose baselines.
2604.12306.paper_summary_zh: 氣候決策在海灣地區越來越需要能夠將異質的科學和政策證據轉化為可行指導的系統，但通用的大型語言模型（LLMs）在區域特定的氣候知識和與地理空間及預測工具的實際互動方面仍然較弱。我們提出了GCA框架，它統一了（i）GCA-DS，一個專注於海灣的精選多模態數據集，以及（ii）海灣氣候代理（GCA），一個增強工具的氣候分析代理。GCA-DS包含約20萬個問題-答案對，涵蓋政府政策和適應計劃、非政府組織和國際框架、學術文獻以及針對熱浪、沙塵暴和洪水的事件驅動報告，並輔以將影像與文本證據結合的遙感輸入。在此基礎上，GCA代理協調了一個模組化的工具管道，基於實時和歷史信號及地理空間處理，產生派生指數和可解釋的視覺化。最後，我們對海灣氣候任務進行了開放和專有LLMs的基準測試，顯示領域微調和工具整合顯著提高了相較於通用基準的可靠性。
2604.12306.author: Muhammad Umer Sheikh et.al.
2604.12306.authors: Muhammad Umer Sheikh, Khawar Shehzad, Salman Khan, Fahad Shahbaz Khan, Muhammad Haris Khan
